content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance Administration          Executive Legal          Enforcement          Conflict of Interest
(916) 327-5660                                 322-5901              322-6441              322-6444


August 22, 1977

                                                                     77-08-203

Patricia Doyle
Attorney at Law
2055 Third Avenue
San Diego, CA 92101

Dear Ms. Doyle:

Pursuant to the request in your letter of August 5, 1977, I'd like
to call your attention to Section 84305 of the Government Code which
states "a copy of every mass mailing in support or in opposition to
a state candidate or state measure shall be sent to the Commission on
the same day on which it is mailed to the public generally." The
Commission does not receive mass mailings in support or in opposition
to any local candidates or measures.

                                             Very truly yours,

                                             [signature]
                                             Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
                                             Technical Assistance
                                             Program Coordinator

RSK/gt


                                                                     27

Patricia M. Doyle
Attorney at Law
2055 Third Avenue
San Diego, California 92101
(714) 296-3429

August 5, 1977

Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Technical Assistance Program Coordinator
State of California Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 507
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Ms. Siciliano-Kutchins,

It is my understanding that the Commission rules require political materials in the way of campaign advertisement and/or mass media advertising be filed with the Fair Political Practices Commission. I would appreciate any help or guidance you could offer in obtaining specific and general campaign advertising for the supervisorial campaign in San Diego for 1976 between Roger Hedgecock--Lou Conde--Nancy Bradshaw.

The parties to the campaign have been less than willing to provide even a small portion of the campaign material requested. It is essential that these materials be provided for various motions related to a case dealing with one of the parties. We would appreciate any help and/or information you could offer in the way of obtaining this information.

Thank you for your time and attention in this matter.

Sincerely yours,

Patricia M. Doyle

PMD/gs
